title: Product Marketing Alignment
summary: Database access for Snowflake Intelligence was improved for creating data models, discussing data access methods and look-alike scoring, culminating in a commitment to build a basic data set.

decisions.description: Build a basic Snowflake table with Dealer ID, name, group, and key CXM fields.
Use CXM as the source of truth for dealer IDs and group IDs.

actionItems.task: Show Matt Watson context for required database access, including dealer groups and CLTV.
Experiment with Snowflake Intelligence and build a basic dealer facts table from CXM.

expectedOutcome.description: Basic table with dealer ID, name, group, inventory count, and historical data.

tensions: High turnover rate in the auto industry (150%) makes data acquisition challenging.
Critical dealer performance KPIs (leads, show rates) are missing.
Invoice tracker data is problematic due to name mismatches and lack of parent group association.
Granular data points are scattered and difficult to obtain.
Existing systems like Clay are perceived as useless and complex.
referencesToPast: Last week we talked about database access.
Past use of ZoomInfo for contacts and emails.
Current system was built independently to capture dealerships.
Invoice tracker data was referenced for dealer information.

contributions.description: Suggested Snowflake Intelligence for external access to simplify data model creation.
Proposed using an LLM to parse websites for email syntax extrapolation.
Provided context on current data approach, including CLTV and churn rates.
Explained the scoring model to find look-alike dealers based on seed groups.
Highlighted the lack of critical performance KPIs for current dealers.
Confirmed use of inference techniques and research APIs for email validity.
Confirmed that the scoring model evolves with new variables and enrichment steps.
